title: A first estimation of gas hydrates offshore Patagonia (Chile)
abstract: The spatial distribution of gas hydrate in the accretionary prism and forearc sediments offshore western Patagonia (50°–57°S), landward of the Antarctica–South America plate boundary, is documented for the first time. BSR-derived heat flow values of 30–70 mW/m² are calculated. Average gas hydrate concentration is 3.4%–10% and average BSR thickness is ~290 m, with estimated methane of ~3.0 × 10¹³ m³.

location.description: Offshore western Patagonia (50°–57°S); first systematic gas hydrate study for southern Chilean Patagonian margin

seismic_lines.name: MCS profiles — offshore Patagonia 50°–57°S (legacy and R/V SONNE)

data.name: MCS Reflection Data — Offshore Patagonia 50°–57°S
data.description: Multichannel seismic reflection profiles from offshore western Patagonia (50°–57°S) used to map BSR distribution and estimate gas hydrate concentration for the first time in this region; BSR-derived heat flow 30–70 mW/m²

data.name: Swath Bathymetry — Offshore Patagonia 50°–57°S
data.description: Multibeam bathymetry from various cruises used to characterize the Patagonian continental margin morphology

_data_note: Open access via OceanRep GEOMAR. First gas hydrate study for the southern Chilean Patagonian margin (50°–57°S).

processing.summary: MCS reflection profiles from offshore western Patagonia (50°–57°S) were analyzed to identify and map the BSR distribution for the first time in this region. Velocity analysis provides gas hydrate concentration estimates (3.4%–10% total volume) and BSR-derived heat flow values (30–70 mW/m²). The large estimated methane volume (~3.0 × 10¹³ m³) makes this an important potential resource.
workflow: 5. Heat flow calculation from BSR depth
processing.notes: Open access via GEOMAR OceanRep repository

analysis_notes: Open access via OceanRep GEOMAR (confirmed URL). First gas hydrate mapping for the Patagonian margin.
